latest.title: Giraffes eat acacia thorns on purpose
latest.script_key: watch those lips that thick tongue it s built to shove acacia thorns into the mouth like a salad shooter that s why they don t bleed those thorns are coated in mucous so thick it clogs the wound did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Fossils turn old bones into time clues
latest.script_key: fossils can preserve a clue from deep time watch the shape in the rock because fossils form when buried remains leave mineral records behind the original body is gone but the pattern can reveal age habitat and behavior which ancient clue should we read next

latest.title: Bears This scent map gives bears away
latest.script_key: bears can smell a meal long before seeing it watch the nose first because smell drives many bear decisions before the eyes do that huge scent map helps them find food avoid danger and read who crossed the area earlier would you trust your nose that much

latest.script_key: coral reefs are built by tiny living builders watch the reef texture because coral animals build hard skeletons that stack into habitat fish algae and countless small creatures then use that structure like a city the color sits on top of engineering which habitat next

latest.title: Reforestation isn't just planting trees
latest.script_key: this cornfield hides a secret watch the hose curl over the rows it s not watering the corn that s why the land s slowly rewilding itself these roots and seeds were already here just waiting for a break did you think reforestation needs a shovel

latest.title: Giraffes don't stretch their necks to reach food
latest.script_key: watch the neck hinge see it it doesn t stretch up that s a myth because giraffes munch sideways not overhead they only tilt their heads down like the rest of us that s why their necks stay straight while they eat did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Goats use wide pupils to watch danger
